title: downingia concolor subsp. brevior
intro: Downingia concolor subsp. brevior, also known as short-spurred Downingia, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its small, white to purple flowers and distinctive short spur. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Downingia concolor subsp. brevior include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. It is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or syrup, and can also be used in poultices or salves to apply topically for localized relief.
